channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Vitol Seals 20-Year, 1MMtpa Deal to Supply LNG to IRH
item.description: Vitol and International Resources Holding finalized a 20-year agreement for Vitol to supply 1 million metric tons per annum of liquefied natural gas to the trading arm of the metals and minerals mining company.

item.title: Vår Energi submits PDO for Gjøa Subsea Projects in North Sea
item.description: Vår Energi and its partners have submitted the PDO for the Ofelia and Gjøa Nord projects in the North Sea to Norway’s Ministry of Energy.

item.title: Wellesley Confirms New Discovery in Norwegian North Sea
item.description: Preliminary calculations for the gas and condensate discovery indicate recoverable resources of 21-107 million barrels of oil equivalent.

item.title: Poland to Build a Third LNG Import Terminal
item.description: 'Today we have the opportunity to create one of the most important gateways to the global LNG market in Central and Eastern Europe'.

item.title: BP, TotalEnergies Join Bab Gas Cap Project in Abu Dhabi
item.description: Expected to produce up to 1.5 billion cubic feet a day of gas, the project represents 'the largest gas cap development of its kind globally', BP said.

item.title: AI Boom Forces Tennessee to Plan Massive Natural Gas Expansion
item.description: The Tennessee Valley Authority released its preliminary 2026 integrated resource plan on Monday, saying load growth in its footprint is already outpacing the reference case forecast in its draft IRP, and that it has…

item.title: UK Energy Secretary Vetoed Plan to Boost Oil Output for Defense Funds
item.description: UK Energy Secretary Ed Miliband has vetoed a plan by the Treasury to boost North Sea oil and gas drilling in order to fund part of Britain’s increased defense funding needs, The Telegraph reported on Thursday, citing a…

item.title: AI Data Center Boom Could Drive Fuel Cell Market to $30 Billion by 2030
item.description: Data center developers are scrambling for reliable power, turning away from congested grids and toward on-site fuel cells. Rystad Energy research and analysis projects a tenfold increase in fuel cell market revenues by…

item.title: MODEC to supply SOFEC mooring system for Coral Norte FLNG
item.description: MODEC said it will supply a SOFEC internal turret mooring system for the Coral Norte floating liquefied natural gas (FLNG) project, which is being developed offshore Mozambique.

item.title: Jadestone starts production from first 2026 Malaysia infill well
item.description: Jadestone Energy has commenced production from the first well in its 2026 infill drilling campaign on the PM323 production sharing contract (PSC) offshore Peninsular Malaysia.

item.title: Middle East Fuel Oil Exports Set to Hit Four-Month High
item.description: Fuel oil exports from the Middle East are expected to rebound to a four-month high this month as tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz picks up and key fuel oil producers Iraq, Saudi Arabia, and Oman have moved to…

item.title: Dangote's Planned IPO Sparks Investor Frenzy in Nigeria
item.description: Dangote Petroleum Refinery and Petrochemicals' plan to raise as much as $2 billion in Africa's biggest IPO has sparked an investor frenzy across Nigeria, drawing interest from some of the country's wealthiest people to…

item.title: Russia Faces Growing Fuel Crunch as Ukrainian Strikes Knock Out Refineries
item.description: Russia is facing its worst nationwide fuel shortages in years, with at least 17 regions imposing mandatory restrictions on gasoline and diesel sales, and dozens of others reporting shortages or restrictions by private…

item.title: Qatar Adds Momentum to Mideast Oil Trade Rebound
item.description: Qatar has joined other Persian Gulf nations in reviving crude oil sales, with regional producers cranking up activity as peace talks between the US and Iran progress.

item.title: ADNOC Cuts Murban Crude Price to $101.48 as Hormuz Tensions Ease
item.description: ADNOC, the national oil company of Abu Dhabi, has cut the official selling price for July for its flagship Murban crude to $101.48 per barrel, down from $104.44 a barrel for June, amid weakening international and Middle…

item.title: TotalEnergies acquires 10% stake in Bab Gas Cap concession
item.description: TotalEnergies entered the Bab Gas Cap concession in Abu Dhabi, with a 10% interest, joining a consortium led by ADNOC, holds a 60% stake.

item.title: China to Increase Fuel Export Allowances for July
item.description: China is raising the volume of fuels it is allowing state refiners to export, in a move that would alleviate concerns about tight refined petroleum supplies in Asia. China’s authorities this week met with executives…

item.title: ADNOC Brings BP, TotalEnergies Into Abu Dhabi’s Biggest Gas Cap Project
item.description: The national oil company of the United Arab Emirates, ADNOC, has signed agreements with BP and TotalEnergies to let the European oil and gas supermajors take 10% each in the consortium developing one of Abu Dhabi’s…

item.title: $70 Oil Could Put India Back on Track for 7% Economic Growth
item.description: India’s economy could return to the trajectory to grow by 7% and even more in the fiscal year through March 2027 if oil prices remain close to the current $70 per barrel, according to a senior official at India’s…

item.title: Iraq Could Quit OPEC in Bid to Pump More Oil
item.description: OPEC’s second-largest producer, Iraq, is considering exiting the cartel if the organization rejects Baghdad’s plan to boost its oil production quota in line with some of the other members, sources in the Iraqi…
